Desarrollar un sistema de registro de turnos para supervisores llamado "Pigmea" que permita capturar y gestionar la siguiente información de producción:

**Campos de registro obligatorios: (mejora el orden según los formularios de este tipo)**
- Máquina: Selector desplegable con opciones (WH1, Giave, WH3, NEXUS, SL2, 21, 22)
- Metros producidos: Campo numérico entero con formato de punto de miles automático (ej: 1.234.567 m)
- Cambios realizados: Campo numérico entero + campo de comentario con autocompletado basado en los comentarios más frecuentes usados históricamente
- Jefe de turno: Selector desplegable con opciones (J.Martín, J.Navarro)
- Turno: Selector desplegable con opciones (Mañana, Tarde, Noche)
- Fecha: Selector de fecha con botón "Hoy" para autocompletar fecha actual o entrada manual

**Requisitos funcionales:**
- Implementar dashboard con informes en tiempo real mostrando métricas de producción por máquina, turno y jefe de turno
- Calcular automáticamente: total de metros por turno, promedio de cambios por máquina, eficiencia por turno, comparativas día vs día anterior
- Generar reportes descargables en formato Excel con formato tabular profesional y PDF con gráficos y tablas resumen
- Sistema debe estar en producción con manejo de errores, logs, respaldos automáticos y autenticación de usuarios
- Interfaz móvil responsive optimizada para tablets y smartphones con botones grandes, navegación intuitiva y sin requiere zoom para operar

**Entregables:**
- Aplicación web desplegada en servidor de producción
- Base de datos con esquema normalizado y respaldos automáticos
- Documentación técnica de API y manual de usuario
- Tests unitarios y de integración con cobertura mínima 80%

**Defining the Application's Focus**

I'm now zeroing in on defining the core functionalities required for this "Pigmea" React application. The emphasis is on creating a robust shift registration system that supervisors can use to track production data efficiently. I'm focusing on key data points like machines, meters, changes, shift bosses, shifts, and dates. My goal is to craft a dashboard that presents essential metrics effectively, too.


**Refining Functional Specs**

I'm now zeroing in on including time calculations, and simulated reports; I have done up some Excel and PDF mockups as well since this is a frontend-only project. Also, the mobile-optimized UI is paramount.


**Integrating AI for Analysis**

I've decided to integrate the Gemini API for a novel "Shift Insight" feature. The primary functionality will remain deterministic, based on CRUD operations, but users can request AI analysis of their production data. I'm focusing on providing concise summaries or anomaly detection capabilities through the AI.

\n\n


**Outlining Application Components**

I've outlined specific React components for the application: a layout shell with navigation, a shift data entry form, a dashboard for visualizations, and a recent records list view. The `iniService.ts` will focus on AI analysis.

I'm now implementing the shift form with a specific ordering for the fields, and formatting for meter inputs. "Changes" will have an autocomplete feature. The dashboard will use `recharts` to display totals and distribution. I'm targeting the `gemini-3-flash-preview` model for prompt analysis and UI/UX with Tailwind.


**Visualizing the Interface**

I'm now establishing the application's visual style. I've chosen an industrial-clean color palette, focusing on slate, blue, and emerald tones. Mobile-first design is a priority, with large touch targets and sticky save buttons. I also created a simple text-based "Pigmea" logo.

I've also begun mental drafting of the application's components. I have boilerplate and the basic types in place. The storage service's structure is coming together with save/get record functions. I've also made a mock data plan to pre-populate the dashboard.


**Updating Data Handling**

I'm now focusing on specific features: CSV downloads will be implemented instead of complex file generation. I'm refining the shift form flow to improve the user experience, focusing on a more logical data entry order. I'm updating the component field order to group context (who, when) followed by the asset (machine) and results (meters/changes).


**Finalizing App Details**

I'm now adding the final touches, like the "Pigmea" title and mobile styling, ensuring the Tailwind classes are correct. A "Generate AI Report" button on the dashboard will initiate AI analysis. Also, the final XML structure is now being constructed, generating specific files with corrections.
